_description: Comprehensive accounting/invoicing system demonstrating entity modeling, relators, computations, events, intelligence artifacts, and normative process-based invoice approval. Tracks invoices, vendors, customers, payments, GL accounts, budgets, and departments with full approval workflows.

description.en: Invoice lifecycle status enumeration
Payment method type enumeration
Tax classification category enumeration
Expense classification category enumeration
General ledger account classification enumeration
Accounts payable or receivable invoice
Invoice line item detail
Supplier or vendor entity
Customer or client entity
Payment transaction record
Organizational department or cost center
General ledger account
Tax rate definition
Department budget allocation
Analytics artifact target for accounting intelligence
Accounts payable clerk handling initial invoice review.
Department manager approval authority for invoices.
Finance director approval authority for escalated invoices.
Chief Financial Officer final approval authority.
4-stage invoice approval process: Initial Review (AP Clerk), Manager Review (Dept Manager), Director Review (Finance Director), CFO Review (CFO)
AP Clerk performs initial review and three-way match verification
Department Manager reviews invoices exceeding $5,000
Finance Director reviews invoices exceeding $25,000
CFO performs final review for invoices exceeding $100,000
Obligation for the AP Clerk to review invoice data and verify three-way match
Obligation for the Department Manager to approve budget allocation
Obligation for the Finance Director to verify vendor history and budget impact
Obligation for the CFO to perform final approval with full authority
Invoice received from vendor
Invoice issued to customer
Invoice contains line item
Invoice charged to department
Payment applied to invoice
Line item posted to GL account
Tax rate applied to line item
Payment method used for a payment
Budget allocated to department
Computed: quantity * unit_price
Computed: line_total * tax_rate
Computed: allocated - spent

property_definitions._comment: --- Invoice Approval Process Properties (base-process-subject-ref-prop inherited from base-process) ---

description.en: Event emitted when a new invoice is created
Event emitted when an invoice is submitted for approval
Event emitted when an invoice is approved
Event emitted when an invoice is rejected
Event emitted when an invoice is fully paid
Event emitted when overdue invoice count exceeds threshold
Event emitted when department spending exceeds budget allocation

computations._comment: === COMPUTATION 1: Line Item Total = quantity * unit_price ===

description.en: Computes line_total = quantity * unit_price on line items

computations.formula: let q = quantity.value; let p = unit_price.value; q * p

description.en: Computes remaining = allocated - spent on budget records

computations.formula: let a = allocated.value; let s = spent.value; a - s

description.en: Publishes notification when invoice status changes

artifact_definitions._comment: === ARTIFACT 1: Spending by Department and Vendor ===
artifact_definitions.name: Spending by Department and Vendor

description.en: Invoice spending aggregated by department and vendor
Line item spending aggregated by GL account, navigating through line item to GL account

artifact_definitions._comment: === ARTIFACT 3: Overdue Invoices by Department (with threshold) ===
artifact_definitions.name: Overdue Invoices by Department

description.en: Overdue invoice count by department with payment overdue threshold

artifact_definitions._comment: === ARTIFACT 4: Budget Utilization by Department (with threshold) ===
artifact_definitions.name: Budget Utilization by Department

description.en: Budget allocation vs spending by department with over-budget threshold
Tax amounts aggregated by department from invoices

artifact_definitions._comment: === ARTIFACT 6: Spending by Department and GL Account ===
artifact_definitions.name: Spending by Department and GL Account

description.en: Line item spending by department (via invoice) and GL account
Invoice spending and tax aggregated by vendor, with count and average invoice size
Payment volumes and amounts aggregated by payment method (wire, check, ACH, etc.)
Comprehensive accounting analytics application with invoice management, vendor tracking, GL account posting, budget monitoring, tax analysis, and normative process-based invoice approval workflows
Core accounting operations: invoices, vendors, customers, payments, GL accounts
Normative process-based invoice approval workflows, authority management, and compliance tracking
Intelligence artifacts, budget monitoring, spending analytics, and tax reporting
